actions-list.actionItem: Pursuant to the order of yesterday, the Journal of the proceedings of the Senate was deemed approved to date.
Pursuant to the order of yesterday, the morning hour being deemed expired, and the times for the recognition of the two leaders being reserved.
The following transactions of morning business occurred on today.

H.R. 6644 (Rep. Hill):
        A bill a bill to increase the supply of housing in America, and for other purposes.

        -- Considered by Senate.

        -- Motion by Senator Thune to commit to Senate Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs with instructions to report back forthwith with the following amendment (SA 4313) fell when cloture was invoked on amendment SA 4308 in Senate.
 

 S.Amdt. 4307 (Sen. Scott, of SC):
        To require the Comptroller General of the United States to submit to Congress studies on workforce housing, housing for persons who are elderly or disabled, proximity of housing to Superfund sites, and residential heirs property.

        --Considered by Senate.


 S.Amdt. 4308 (Sen. Scott, of SC):
        In the nature of a substitute.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --Cloture invoked in Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 89 - 9. Record Vote Number:50  



 S.Amdt. 4310 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.


 S.Amdt. 4311 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.


 S.Amdt. 4312 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.


 S.Amdt. 4313 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --SA 4313 (the instructions of the motion to commit) fell when cloture invoked SA 4308.


 S.Amdt. 4314 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --SA 4314 fell when SA 4313 (the instructions of the motion to commit) fell.


 S.Amdt. 4315 (Sen. Thune):
        To improve the bill.

        --Considered by Senate.

        --SA 4315 fell when SA 4314 fell.




 H.R. 7147 (Rep. Cole):
        A bill making further consolidated appropriations for the fiscal year ending September 30, 2026, and for other purposes.

        -- Motion to proceed to consideration of measure made in Senate.

        -- Third cloture motion on the motion to proceed to the measure presented in Senate.


 S. 327 (Sen. Cortez Masto):
        A bill to amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to deny any foreign tax credit or deduction with respect to taxes paid or accrued to the Russian Federation.

        -- Senate Committee on Finance discharged by Unanimous Consent.

        -- Passed Senate without amendment by Unanimous Consent.

PN711:
        Lt. Gen. Joshua M. Rudd, in the Army, to be General

        -- Considered by Senate.

        -- Confirmed by the Senate by Yea-Nay Vote. 71 - 29. Record Vote Number: 49
By unanimous consent, on the request of Mr. Kennedy, at  5:53 p.m., the Senate adjourned, under its order of today, until 10 a.m. tomorrow.
